get.description:
Returns information for each email that matches the search parameter(s).
If the search takes too long, the endpoint returns 202 with a Location header
pointing to a polling endpoint where results can be retrieved once ready.

parameters.description:
The beginning of the search date range.
Defaults to `now - 30 days` if not provided.
parameters.name: start

parameters.description:
The end of the search date range.
Defaults to `now` if not provided.
parameters.name: end

parameters.description:
The space-delimited term used in the query. The search is case-insensitive.

The content of the following email metadata fields are searched:
* alert_id
* CC
* From (envelope_from)
* From Name
* final_disposition
* md5 hash (of any attachment)
* sha1 hash (of any attachment)
* sha256 hash (of any attachment)
* name (of any attachment)
* Reason
* Received DateTime (yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss)
* Sent DateTime (yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss)
* ReplyTo
* To (envelope_to)
* To Name
* Message-ID
* smtp_helo_server_ip
* smtp_previous_hop_ip
* x_originating_ip
* Subject
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Determines if the search results will include detections or not.
parameters.name: detections_only

parameters.description: Determines if the message action log is included in the response.
parameters.name: action_log

parameters.description: The dispositions the search filters by.
parameters.name: final_disposition

parameters.description: The message actions the search filters by.
parameters.name: message_action

parameters.description: Filter by recipient. Matches either an email address or a domain.
parameters.name: recipient

parameters.description: Filter by sender. Matches either an email address or a domain.
parameters.name: sender

parameters.description: Filter by a domain found in the email: sender domain, recipient domain, or a domain in a link.
parameters.name: domain

parameters.description: Search for messages containing individual keywords in any order within the subject.
parameters.name: subject

parameters.description: Search for messages with an exact subject match.
parameters.name: exact_subject

parameters.description: Search for submissions instead of original messages
parameters.name: submissions

parameters.description: The number of results per page.
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Deprecated: Use cursor pagination instead.
parameters.name: page

200.description: Contains the search results for the provided query.

202.description: The search is taking longer than expected. Use the Location header to poll for results.

Location.description: URL to poll for search results

post.description: Maximum batch size: 1000 messages per request

ids.description: List of message IDs to move.

postfix_ids.description: Deprecated: Use `ids` instead. List of message IDs to move.

post.description:
Generates a preview of an email message for safe viewing without executing any
embedded content.

screenshot.description: A base64 encoded PNG image of the email.

post.description: Releases a quarantined email message, allowing it to be delivered to the recipient.

schema.description: A list of messages identfied by their `postfix_id`s that should be released.

get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific email message, including headers,
metadata, and security scan results.

detection_reasons: Selector is a source of spam/uce : Smtp-Helo-Server-Ip=127.0.0[dot]186
example.subject: listen, I highly recommend u to read that email, just to ensure not a thing will take place

get.description: Returns detection details such as threat categories and sender information for non-benign messages.

headers.name: Subject
headers.value: listen, I highly recommend u to read that email, just to ensure not a thing will take place

as_name.description: The name of the autonomous system.

as_number.description: The number of the autonomous system.

post.description: Moves a single email message to a different folder or changes its quarantine status.

get.description: Returns a preview of the message body as a base64 encoded PNG image for non-benign messages.

screenshot.description: A base64 encoded PNG image of the email.

200.description: Contains a preview of the email.

get.description: Returns the raw eml of any non-benign message.

example.raw:
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"

From: sender@example.com
To: recipient@example.com
Subject: Test Email

This is a test email.

raw.description: A UTF-8 encoded eml file of the email.

200.description: Contains the raw content of the email.

post.description:
Submits an email message for reclassification, updating its threat assessment
based on new analysis.

eml_content.description: Base64 encoded content of the EML file

get.description:
Gets the delivery trace for an email message, showing its path through email
security processing.

Retrieves `PhishGuard` reports showing phishing attempts and suspicious email patterns
detected.

parameters.description: The beginning of the search date range (RFC3339 format).
parameters.name: start

parameters.description: The end of the search date range (RFC3339 format).
parameters.name: end

is_acceptable_sender.description:
Messages from this sender will be exempted from Spam, Spoof and Bulk dispositions.
Note: This will not exempt messages with Malicious or Suspicious dispositions.

is_exempt_recipient.description: Messages to this recipient will bypass all detections.

is_trusted_sender.description: Messages from this sender will bypass all detections and link following.

verify_sender.description:
Enforce DMARC, SPF or DKIM authentication.
When on, Email Security only honors policies that pass authentication.

400.description: Invalid request, generally because of the format/content of the email send request. No email will be sent when this happens.

403.description: Email sending is disabled for this zone/account.

429.description: Account/zone has surpassed the rate at which it can send email, please try again later. No email will be sent when this happens.

500.description: An unexpected error while processing the email send request. No email will be sent when this happens.

get.description: Get information about the settings for your Email Routing zone.

patch.description: Unlock MX Records previously locked by Email Routing.

Enable you Email Routing zone. Add and lock the necessary MX and SPF records.

get.description: Lists existing routing rules.

schema.description: Maximum number of results per page.

post.description: Rules consist of a set of criteria for matching emails (such as an email being sent to a specific custom email address) plus a set of actions to take on the email (like forwarding it to a specific destination address).

get.description: Get information on the default catch-all routing rule.

put.description: Enable or disable catch-all routing rule, or change action to forward to specific destination address.

get.description: Get information for a specific routing rule already created.

put.description: Update actions and matches, or enable/disable specific routing rules.

get.description: Lists all sending-enabled subdomains for the zone.

post.description: Creates a new sending subdomain or re-enables sending on an existing subdomain that had it disabled. If zone-level Email Sending has not been enabled yet, the zone flag is automatically set when the entitlement is present.

Returns the DNS records that would be created for a sending subdomain, flags which records are missing, and reports any conflicts with existing DNS records. This is a read-only dry-run — no records are created or modified. Use before or after creating a subdomain to check DNS status.

delete.description: Disables sending on a subdomain and removes its DNS records. If routing is still active on the subdomain, only sending is disabled.

get.description: Gets information for a specific sending subdomain.
Returns the expected DNS records for a sending subdomain.

200.description: Get sending subdomain DNS records response

Select to prevent recently registered domains from triggering a
Suspicious or Malicious disposition.

Select for partner or other approved domains that have similar
spelling to your connected domains. Prevents listed domains from
triggering a Spoof disposition.

email_dns_record.description: List of records needed to enable an Email Routing zone.

name.description: DNS record name (or @ for the zone apex).

priority.description: Required for MX, SRV and URI records. Unused by other record types. Records with lower priorities are preferred.

ttl.description: Time to live, in seconds, of the DNS record. Must be between 60 and 86400, or 1 for 'automatic'.

example.mime_message:
From: sender@example.com
To: recipient@example.com
Subject: Hello
Content-Type: text/plain

Hello, World!

mime_message.description: The full MIME-encoded email message. Should include standard RFC 5322 headers such as From, To, Subject, and Content-Type. The from and recipients fields in the request body control SMTP envelope routing; the From and To headers in the MIME message control what the recipient's email client displays.
mime_message.example:
From: sender@example.com
To: recipient@example.com
Subject: Hello
Content-Type: text/plain

Hello, World!
